meta.description: Personal health and training database — Todd Blackhurst AI coaching system

oura_biology_baselines.notes: Oura Biology personal baselines. VO2 Max 38.9 (Below Average — cardio fitness improvement goal). HRV baseline 32.5 ms (any reading below this signals recovery deficit). Avg sleep 6h 25m/night — chronic mild sleep debt.

nutrition_targets.notes: Targets set 2026-04-11. ~380 kcal/day deficit (between mild and moderate). Protein elevated for anabolic resistance at age 57. Fat raised for hormone support. Recalculate if weight changes ±3 kg or training frequency shifts.

body_composition.notes: Baseline from Bevel 30-day Progress Report (Nov 5 – Nov 24)
End-point from Bevel 30-day Progress Report — marked as Low on fat distribution
Bevel weekly scan (week of Apr 6). Visceral Fat Level 2.0. BF% 16.2%, weight 75.9 kg / 167.3 lbs. Primary VAT reduction target ongoing.

Hume Body Pod full scan, updated Apr 10. Air-displacement plethysmography (higher BF% than BIA/Bevel). Trunk muscle Low — core/anti-rotation priority confirmed. Bilateral arm fat High. Goal weight 165 lbs.

Bevel morning scan Apr 11, 4:29 AM. Weight stable at 166.0 lbs. Body fat 17.4% — excellent control. Lean mass 137.1 lbs (78.1% muscle) — preserved well. Visceral fat Lv 3.0 indicates modest VAT. BMR 1779 kcal baseline for deficit calculations.

Bevel morning scan Apr 12, 6:47 AM. Weight 165.8 lbs — down 0.2 lbs from Apr 11. BF 16.3% stable. Lean mass 138.8 lbs (79.1%). Visceral Fat Lv 2.0 (down from 3.0 on Apr 11 — positive signal). BMR 1779 kcal consistent. Trending toward 165 lb goal.

body_composition.body_fat_note: BIA underestimate — not reliable for body fat tracking; use Bevel and Hume ADP pod scan as primary reference
body_composition.notes: Hume Pod BIA daily scale Apr 12, 6:51 AM. Weight 166.8 lbs. Metabolic Age 42 yrs (Excellent). Health Score 673. Trunk muscle Low (consistent with ADP scan Apr 10). BF 10.2% is a BIA artifact — ADP scan (Apr 10) measured 22.0%. Segmental swings vs Apr 10 are hydration-driven, not real muscle change. Trunk Low rating structurally persistent.

Ocare3 BIA scan Apr 13, 06:11 AM. Weight 165.6 lbs — essentially AT goal weight (165 lbs). BF 15.7% (BIA — use Bevel/Hume ADP as primary reference). Muscle 79.6%, Visceral Fat Lv 2.0 stable. BMR 1778 kcal consistent. Last week avg 165.6, max 166.0, min 164.9 — trend holding at goal range.

Hume Pod daily Apr 13, 6:43 AM. Weight 166.8 lbs. Weekly avg 165.6 lbs — within 1 lb of 165 lb goal. Health Score 673 High, Biological Age 30 yrs (vs chronological 57). Steps 5,230 by 6:43 AM, Active Cal 261 (day ongoing). BF% from BIA not reliable — use ADP scan Apr 10 (22.0%) as reference.

recovery_sleep.notes: Low strain day — Oura. Productive Sleep Debt Recovery noted. Outdoor cycling session same morning.
Sleep Quality 55%, below average. Time in Bed 7h 43m, Asleep 7h 17m. Wind-down 9:20 PM, target bedtime 9:50 PM.
Recovery 58% Normal. Sleep 73% Above normal. Deep Sleep 59m (notable). HR Dip 14%. Sleep Bank -1h 49m. Cardio Load 29 Detraining. Sleep HRV avg 31ms / max 126ms. Lowest HR 56 bpm.
Significant HRV Drop — Recovery crashed to 26% (HRV 19.3 ms, ~20ms below baseline). Despite 1h 6min deep sleep. Sleep started Apr 3 at 10:19 PM. Reduction in training intensity indicated.
Recovery Breakthrough — HRV surged to 51.4 ms (+52% rebound from Apr 4's 26%). Nervous system adapting well to training. Sleep 5h 50m (started Apr 4 at 10:56 PM). Sleep Score 69 FAIR.

Recovery Slide 41% — HRV 36.2 ms (Recovery metric), 25 ms avg overnight. HRV/HR spike after Apr 8 high-strain session. Sleep Score 69 FAIR. 5h 28m asleep / 7h 4m in bed (9:21 PM – 4:25 AM). Efficiency 77%. Deep 38m, REM 65m. HR Dip 15%. Debt -2h 13m.

Resilience Signal — steady 57% recovery despite Apr 10 high-strain session. HR dropped 3.1 bpm. Rise: 6h 48m sleep / 8h 13m in bed (9:18 PM – 5:31 AM), Efficiency 83%, Deep 43m, REM 1h 18m, Latency 22m, Restfulness Good, Timing Optimal, Debt -2h 30m Moderate. AutoSleep: 7h 14m total, REM 2h 6m, Deep 30m, Debt -2h 8m, Sleep Time 9:38 PM. HR Dip 14%. Lowest HR 58. HRV avg 26ms / max 151ms. Avg HR 64. SpO2 95.2%.

Recovery Breakthrough — Oura 68% (Above normal, +12% from yesterday). Rise sleep score 70/GOOD. REM above normal at 2h 28m (Oura) / 1h 2m (Rise). HRV 41.7ms above personal baseline of 32.5ms. Sleep short at 6h 15m–6h 54m. Moderate sleep debt (~2h 12m). Latency 37m — attributed to elevated metabolism from prior workout. Restfulness flagged. Respiratory rate slightly below normal (14.1 rpm) — monitor. SpO2 96.1% normal. Training guidance: Amber-Green, full session OK, HR <122 bpm. | Motra supplement: HRV Stabilizing (7d +1.6ms, 30d -3.8ms), RHR 62.7 bpm Stable, VO2 Max 39.6 Good (+4.9 over 90d), Weight 166.3 lbs (30d high, +0.8 lbs/wk trend). Training status: Amber-Green confirmed.

Positives: deep sleep 59min/18% solid, restfulness Optimal, HR stable. Fasted morning walk already done (5:16 AM, 2.43mi, Zone 0-1, 16% Activity Strain). Training guidance: Amber — proceed with programmed strength session, HR cap 122 bpm, treat first sets as warm-in, reduce Block C if fatigue surfaces.

Week 1 Monday — Pull + Glute Hinge. Added Pull-Ups (unplanned) and Wall Slides to PREP. 80 min session — OVER 65 min cap. Volume 6.3K kg / 475 kcal. HR context: Amber day (HRV 20ms, below 32.5ms baseline).

Indoor Cycle Apr 14, Tanzi District. 31:10, avg HR 100 bpm — Easy effort. Second movement session of the day (following 5:53 AM outdoor walk). HR well within <122 bpm constraint. Total active calories today: 371 (walk 187 + cycle 184). Double cardio on active recovery day — volume appropriate given Easy effort on both sessions.

bullets: HRV 51.3ms — strongest reading of the entire block. +45% from yesterday's 35.4ms, 57% above 32.5ms baseline. Two consecutive early bedtimes (9:01 PM → 9:36 PM) drove HRV from 20ms → 35.4ms → 51.3ms across 3 nights. Dose-response confirmed.
Sleep: 6h51m asleep, efficiency 97% (Bevel Efficiency Breakthrough flag). Oura sleep score 79 Good — optimal timing. Sleep bank -2h52m, improving from -3h13m. Deep sleep 37–54m, REM 1h6m–1h50m.
SpO2 cleared to 96.5% Normal (from 95.1% yesterday). Wrist temp 97.5°F normalized (from 96.9°F flag). RHR 61.9 bpm (Bevel) / 59 bpm (Oura) — excellent. Cardio load 26 Maintaining.
Readiness verdict: Green. Push your strength session. This is the HRV signal you've been building toward all week.

training_direction.call: Wednesday Push + Quad / Anti-Rotation — HRV 51.3ms Green. Push it.
bullets: Incline Press opens at 16kg/hand. All 10 reps clean across 3 sets → flag for 18kg next session. Half-Kneeling Landmine Press: left side leads every set, right-side dominance will show up.
Block A (Floor 2): Incline DB Press + Half-Kneeling Landmine Press superset. Block B: Split Squat + Cable Lateral Raise. Block C: Pallof Press + Serratus Punches + Copenhagen Hip Adduction.
Timer starts BEFORE first prep exercise. Block C only if Block B closes before 55-min mark. Five sessions in a row you've run over 65 min. That ends today.
HR cap: 122 bpm throughout. Rest 60–75 sec between sets. This is strength work, not conditioning. Protect the recovery you built this week.

session_plan.session_type: NEXT: Push + Quad / Anti-Rotation — Wednesday Apr 15
session_plan.session_goal: Develop horizontal and vertical push strength; reinforce quad-dominant unilateral pattern with hip-OA-safe depth; anti-rotation and shoulder correction to close.
session_plan.readiness_call: TBD — check Wednesday morning HRV. Today at 35.4ms (Green) — strong baseline going in if sleep holds.
session_plan.notes: Timer starts BEFORE first prep exercise. If Block C not started by 55-min mark — skip it. Compounds first, accessories last.

exercises.name: Wall Slide
exercises.note: Back, head, and BOTH elbows flat against wall throughout. Anterior chain tightness correction before any pressing. Slow down when contact breaks — don't power through the restriction.

exercises.name: Hip Flexor Stretch (Kneeling)
exercises.note: Left hip flexor is typically tighter — add 10s on the left. Half-kneeling. Anterior chain release before split squat work. Hip OA: no forced ROM.

exercises.name: TRX Y-Raise
exercises.note: Lower-trap activation before pressing. Lean back ~45°, arms form Y overhead. 3-sec controlled lowering. Corrects shoulder elevation pattern that appears under pressing load.

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.note: Lower back pinned to floor — non-negotiable. Alternating arm + opposite leg extension. Anti-rotation primer before landmine press. Reduce ROM before breaking back contact.

blocks.label: Floor 2 — Block A: Vertical + Horizontal Push (~18 min)

exercises.name: Half-Kneeling Landmine Press
exercises.load: ~15–20 kg total (bar + plate) — build across sets
exercises.note: Left knee down, right foot forward. Diagonal pressing pattern — shoulder-friendly, unilateral correction built in. Left side sets the standard. Right-side dominance will show up — let left lead.

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Press
exercises.load: 16 kg/hand — if all 10 reps clean → 18 kg next session
exercises.note: 30–45° incline. LEFT side initiates — right-side dominance shows up in pressing. 3-sec lowering tempo. If rep 9–10 starts to grind, stop at 8.

blocks.label: Floor 2 — Block B: Quad Dominant + Unilateral (~16 min)

exercises.name: Hammer Strength V-Squat (Limited Depth)
exercises.load: BW or light plate — add load only if depth stays hip-OA-safe
exercises.note: Stop before hip crease reaches knee — hip OA protocol. Equal drive through both feet. Do not chase depth. Feet hip-width, slight toe-out.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Split Squat
exercises.note: Left leg forward, right leg back — left leads every set. Hip OA: control descent, no forced depth. Left side is typically weaker — let it set pace.

blocks.label: Floor 2 — Block C: Anti-Rotation / Shoulder Close (~14 min)
blocks.notes: SKIP if over 55-min mark when you arrive here. Compounds > accessories always.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.note: Shoulder imbalance correction. LEFT side first every set. Strict control — no momentum. Elbow slightly bent throughout. Rear delt and mid-delt emphasis.

exercises.name: Pallof Press (Unilateral / Anti-Rotation)
exercises.note: You called this 'fun' and sweated hard doing it — that's the right intensity. Anti-rotation is the core's actual job. Press out, hold 2 seconds, return. Do not rotate — that's the whole point.

exercises.name: KB Goblet Carry Walk + Squat
exercises.note: Goblet position — KB held at chest. Walk 15m, then 5 goblet squats (hip-OA-safe depth). Core braced throughout. Fun, athletic finish to the session.

coaching_brief.nutrition_binding_constraint: Pre-workout shake + drink queued (not yet logged): ~446 kcal / 56g P / 46g C / 7g F. Once logged: 1,554 kcal / 94g P / 144g C / 63g F remaining for the day. Fat is the binding constraint at 63g remaining — that is the entire budget minus shake. Zero oil at lunch and dinner. Post-session shake adds ~40g P / ~7g F — that takes fat to 14g with ~56g remaining for two meals. Manageable but tight.

Stage a lean snack (jerky or whey) before leaving the gym. Fat budget is tight today — any unplanned snack with fat will close the budget before dinner.
